# Boggart Boneboiler

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Boneboiler |
| **Description** | A bloated, greasy humanoid with slick skin and fat folds glistening under firelight. It reeks of burnt fat, spoiled meat, and sour sweat. Clad in a stained leather apron stretched tight over its bulk, it lumbers about with wet, slapping steps. Its small, beady eyes gleam with cruel delight as it tends to bubbling pots filled with bones and scraps, occasionally dragging victims closer to “the boil.” |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (leather apron) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 + 2 |
| **Strength** | 12 (+1) |
| **Constitution** | 12 (+1) |

| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |

Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler lunges at (insert target) with a meat hook!* **Hurl Hot Grease:** +2 to hit, range 3, 2d4 fire damage; **recharge 6** (roll a d6 at the start of its turn and regain use on a 6); target is **Blinded** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler hurls a pot of hot grease at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Polished Finger Bone** (15%) |
| **Comments** | “Into the pot with ya!”, “Boil ’em slow, tastes better!”, “Oi, don’t waste the juices!”, “This one’ll make a fine broth!”, “Keep stirrin’, lads!”, “Boss wants this one salted!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Boneboiler has a chance to drop:
- **Polished Finger Bone** — 15%

### Polished Finger Bone Summary
A smooth, yellowed finger bone, carefully cleaned and buffed to a dull shine.
- non-magical
- worth 25 gp

### XP Note
The submitted stat block listed **25 XP**, but this entry uses **50 XP** to match the current Umberhold experience table for a **Level 2** monster.

# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

# Boggart Knifer

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Knifer |
| **Description** | A small, wiry humanoid with patchy, grimy skin and oversized ears. Its eyes gleam with sly hunger, and its yellowed teeth jut out in a crooked grin. It smells of damp earth, sour sweat, and old blood. Clad in mismatched scraps of leather and cloth, it creeps and twitches constantly, muttering under its breath and circling foes like a scavenger sizing up a carcass. |
| **Level** | 1 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 13 (base 10 + Agility + leather armor) |
| **Hit Points** | 1d6 |
| **Strength** | 10 (+0) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 12 (+1) |
| **Intellect** | 8 (-1) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 8 (-1) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Agility +3 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | Stealth +3 |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Rusty Dagger:** +3 to hit, range 1, 1d4 piercing. Combat text: *Boggart Knifer stabs at (insert target) with a rusty dagger!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Boggart Cloak** (1 in 500) |
| **Comments** | “Get stuck in, lads!”, “Oi, this one’s got meat on ’em!”, “Stab it in the hammy so it can’t run!”, “Heh, I’m keepin’ the good bits!”, “Don’t break it—boss said fresh!” |
| **Experience** | 25 |

---

## Notes

### Unique Rare Drop
In addition to normal loot, a Boggart Knifer has a rare chance to drop:
- **Boggart Cloak** — 1 in 500

### Boggart Cloak Summary
A patchwork cloak of filthy cloth, flayed skin, and scraps of wulf fur, reeking of damp rot.
- non-magical
- grants advantage on Stealth checks
- weight: 1 stone

# Wulf

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Wulf |
| **Description** | A small, mangy beast with patchy fur and a hunched, underfed frame. Its ribs press against its skin, and its eyes glint with mean, hungry intelligence. Its fangs are yellowed and rotten, and its breath is foul with the stench of decay. It reeks of wet fur, rot, and filth. arguments.command:
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p umberhold/monsters/5E
for file in umberhold/monsters/*.md; do
  base=$(basename "$file" .md)
  if [ "$base" = "README" ]; then
    continue
  fi
  mkdir -p "umberhold/monsters/5E/$base"
  cp "$file" "umberhold/monsters/5E/$base/$base.md"
done
find umberhold/monsters/5E -maxdepth 2 -type f | sort

# Umberhold Monster Stat Block Canon

This document captures the current approved generic monster stat block for Umberhold.

It defines the baseline fields used to build monster entries.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. Core Monster Fields

| Field | Details |
|---|---|
| Name | Name of the creature |
| Description | Describes the creature in detail, including appearance, odor, demeanor, behavior, and other sensory notes |
| Level | 1-20 |
| Size | Small, Medium, Large, Huge, or Gigantic |
| Type | Beast, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Insect, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, or Undead |
| Alignment | Good, Neutral, or Evil |
| AC | Base 10 when unarmored, modified by Agility, armor, and other effects |
| Hit Points | Determined by size and level |
| Strength | 3-30 |
| Constitution | 3-30 |
| Agility | 3-30 |
| Intellect | 3-30 |
| Piety | 3-30 |
| Personality | 3-30 |
| Luck | 3-30 |
| Proficiency Bonus | Based on level progression |
| Saving Throw Proficiencies | List of proficient saves |
| Skill Proficiencies | List of proficient skills |
| Damage Vulnerabilities | List of damage vulnerabilities |
| Damage Resistances | List of damage resistances |
| Damage Immunities | List of damage immunities |
| Condition Immunities | List of condition immunities |
| Attacks | List of attacks, including to-hit, damage, damage type, and special effects |
| Treasure Type | Treasure category from A-Z, based on treasure tables |
| Comments | Optional creature comments, including a 5% chance at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type |
| Experience | XP awarded to the party for slaying the monster |

---

## 2. Monster Level

Monsters use a **Level** value rather than an external challenge rating style label.

Current monster level range:
- **1-20**

---

## 3. Monster Size

Monster size categories are:
- Small
- Medium
- Large
- Huge
- Gigantic

### Combat Occupancy Mapping
Under Umberhold's current combat system, size maps to enemy rank occupancy as follows:
- Small = 1 slot
- Medium = 1 slot
- Large = 2 slots
- Huge = 3 slots
- Gigantic = 4 slots

---

## 4. Monster Types

The current approved monster types are:
- Beast
- Dragon
- Elemental
- Fey
- Fiend
- Giant
- Humanoid
- Insect
- Monstrosity
- Ooze
- Plant
- Undead

---

## 5. Alignment

The current approved alignment values are:
- Good
- Neutral
- Evil

---

## 6. Armor Class

Monster AC uses a base of **10** when unarmored.

AC may then be modified by:
- armor
- Agility bonus
- magical effects
- other special modifiers

---

## 7. Hit Point Progression by Size

Monster hit points are based on size.

| Size | Hit Dice per Level |
|---|---|
| Small | 1d6 per level |
| Medium | 1d8 per level |
| Large | 1d12 per level |
| Huge | 2d10 per level |
| Gigantic | 2d20 per level |

---

## 8. Core Attributes

All monsters use the same seven core attributes as player characters:
- Strength
- Constitution
- Agility
- Intellect
- Piety
- Personality
- Luck

Each attribute currently ranges from:
- **3-30**

---

## 9. Proficiency Bonus by Level

| Level Range | Proficiency Bonus |
|---|---|
| 1-3 | +2 |
| 4-6 | +3 |
| 7-9 | +4 |
| 10-12 | +5 |
| 13-15 | +6 |
| 16-18 | +7 |
| 19-20 | +8 |

---

## 10. Saving Throw and Skill Proficiencies

Monsters may have:
- saving throw proficiencies
- skill proficiencies

If a monster is proficient in a save or skill, it adds its proficiency bonus to the relevant roll.

---

## 11. Damage and Condition Defenses

Monster entries may include:
- damage vulnerabilities
- damage resistances
- damage immunities
- condition immunities

These should use Umberhold's current damage types and condition list.

---

## 12. Attacks

A monster's attack list should include, at minimum:
- attack name
- to-hit bonus
- damage
- damage type
- range
- any attached effects or conditions

### Attack Range Rule
Every monster attack must have a **range value from 1-3**.

---

## 13. Treasure, Comments, and Experience

### Treasure Type
Each monster may include a **Treasure Type** field from **A-Z**, based on Umberhold's treasure tables.

### Comments
Each monster may include a **Comments** field.

This currently supports flavor such as:
- a **5% chance** at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type

### Experience
Each monster should include an **Experience** value showing how much XP is awarded to the party for slaying it.

The exact XP values by monster level are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

---

## 14. Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- monsters use Level rather than challenge rating
- current monster levels are 1-20
- monsters use the five size categories Small, Medium, Large, Huge, and Gigantic
- Small and Medium occupy 1 combat slot
- Large occupies 2 combat slots
- Huge occupies 3 combat slots
- Gigantic occupies 4 combat slots
- the current monster type list is Beast, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Insect, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, and Undead
- the current alignment values are Good, Neutral, and Evil
- unarmored AC starts at 10
- hit point dice scale by size category
- monsters use the same seven core attributes as player characters
- monster attributes currently range from 3-30
- proficiency bonus follows the current level progression table
- monster entries include a Description field
- monster entries include saving throw proficiencies and skill proficiencies
- monster entries may include damage vulnerabilities, damage resistances, damage immunities, and condition immunities
- monster entries include attacks and XP value
- monster entries may include Treasure Type and Comments

---

## 15. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- whether monster hit points use only size dice or also add Constitution-derived bonuses by level
- whether alignment has any systemic effect beyond lore and spell interaction
- the full canonical skill list used by monsters and characters
- whether monster spellcasters follow player spell slot rules or use bespoke spellcasting packages

---

## 16. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

# Umberhold Experience Canon

This document captures the current approved rules for:
- monster XP values by monster level
- XP distribution to the party
- level-up access at inns

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. Experience Awards from Monsters

When a monster is slain, it grants experience based on its **monster level**.

### Experience by Monster Level

| Level | XP |
|---|---:|
| 1 | 25 |
| 2 | 50 |
| 3 | 100 |
| 4 | 200 |
| 5 | 400 |
| 6 | 600 |
| 7 | 800 |
| 8 | 1,200 |
| 9 | 1,600 |
| 10 | 2,400 |
| 11 | 3,200 |
| 12 | 4,200 |
| 13 | 5,400 |
| 14 | 6,800 |
| 15 | 8,500 |
| 16 | 10,500 |
| 17 | 13,000 |
| 18 | 16,000 |
| 19 | 19,500 |
| 20 | 23,500 |

---

## 2. XP Distribution

Experience gained by the party is:
- awarded when monsters are slain
- shared equally among **surviving party members**

Characters who are not surviving members at the end of the encounter do **not** receive a share of that encounter's experience.

---

## 3. Leveling Up

When a character has enough experience to advance to the next level:
- they must go to the nearest **inn**
- they choose the option to **advance**

### Level-Up Cost
- there is **no gold cost** to level up

### Note
The full level-up rules are still to be detailed separately.

---

## 4. Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- monsters award XP based on monster level
- the XP table for monster levels 1-20 is defined
- XP is shared equally among surviving party members
- leveling up is performed at an inn
- leveling up has no gold cost
- detailed level-up rules will be defined separately

---

## 5. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- the exact character XP thresholds required for levels 1-10
- whether benched roster members ever receive shared XP
- whether summoned allies or temporary NPC allies affect XP splitting
- whether story or quest XP exists in addition to monster XP
- whether escape or partial-victory outcomes grant reduced XP
- the exact level-up benefits by class and level

---

## 6. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

# Boggart Gasser

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Gasser |
| **Description** | A stunted, pot-bellied humanoid swaddled in grime-stiff wrappings and a patchwork hood dark with old soot. Its slick skin is blotched with yellow lesions, and its nostrils are stuffed with greasy moss to blunt the fumes it brews. Glass vials clink from cords across its chest, each one sloshing with corpse-green vapor and rancid alchemical slurry. It reeks of rot, vinegar, burst lungs, and the sweet-sour taint of spoiled herbs left to steep in blood. When it grins, its blackened teeth show through cracked lips, and it cackles as it lobs choking death into clustered foes before scampering back into the murk. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 13 (base 10 + Agility + patchwork leathers) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 8 (-1) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 14 (+2) |
| **Intellect** | 10 (+0) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 8 (-1) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Agility +4 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | Stealth +4 |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Jagged Shank:** +1 to hit, range 1, 1d4-1 piercing. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser darts in and jabs (insert target) with a jagged shank!* **Poison Gas Vial:** +4 to hit, range 3, 1d4 piercing + 1d4 poison; target must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or become **Poisoned** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser hurls a glass vial of poisonous gas at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Stoppered Gas Vial** (10%) |
| **Comments** | “Breathe deep, pretty thing!”, “Cough for me!”, “Rot in your chest, slow and wet!”, “Glass for the lungs, gas for the eyes!”, “Heh—listen to ’em choke!”, “Boss says don’t waste the good fumes!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Gasser has a chance to drop:
- **Stoppered Gas Vial** — 10%

### Stoppered Gas Vial Summary
A cloudy glass vial stoppered with black wax, still swirling with a foul green vapor that stings the eyes.
- non-magical
- worth 30 gp

### Tactics Note
Boggart Gassers prefer to fight from the rear ranks, hurling poison into weakened or tightly packed enemies while larger boggarts keep victims pinned in place.

arguments.content:
# Marrow Muncher

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Marrow Muncher |
| **Description** | A towering, thick-limbed brute with tangled, filthy hair and slabs of muscle beneath dirt-caked skin. Its gut hangs heavy over a crude belt strung with cracked skulls, trophies taken and later used as missiles. The creature reeks of unwashed flesh and old rot, its presence announced by stench before sight. Small, dim eyes peer out with dull hunger as it lumbers forward, dragging an oversized club scarred from countless beatings. |
| **Level** | 3 |
| **Size** | Large (2 squares on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Giant |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (base 10 + Agility -1 + thick hide) |
| **Hit Points** | 3d12 + 9 |
| **Strength** | 18 (+4) |
| **Constitution** | 17 (+3) |
| **Agility** | 8 (-1) |
| **Intellect** | 6 (-2) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 7 (-2) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Strength +6, Constitution +5 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Oversized Club:** +6 to hit, range 2, 1d10 + 4 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *The Marrow Muncher swings its massive club at (insert target)!* **Thrown Skull:** +6 to hit, range 3, 1d10 + 4 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *The Marrow Muncher hurls a skull from its grisly belt at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | C; **Skull Belt** (1 in 200) |
| **Comments** | “Smash you flat!”, “Bones mine—me keep!”, “Stop wrigglin’!”, “Heh... you break easy!”, “I eat after fight...” |
| **Experience** | 100 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Marrow Muncher has a rare chance to drop:
- **Skull Belt** — 1 in 200

### Skull Belt Summary
A crude belt strung with cracked and weathered skulls, still faintly reeking of death.
- non-magical
- grants advantage on Intimidation checks
- weight: 4 stone

# Boggart Gasser

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Gasser |
| **Description** | A stunted, pot-bellied humanoid swaddled in grime-stiff wrappings and a patchwork hood dark with old soot. Its slick skin is blotched with yellow lesions, and its nostrils are stuffed with greasy moss to blunt the fumes it brews. Glass vials clink from cords across its chest, each one sloshing with corpse-green vapor and rancid alchemical slurry. It reeks of rot, vinegar, burst lungs, and the sweet-sour taint of spoiled herbs left to steep in blood. When it grins, its blackened teeth show through cracked lips, and it cackles as it lobs choking death into clustered foes before scampering back into the murk. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 13 (base 10 + Agility + patchwork leathers) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 8 (-1) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 14 (+2) |
| **Intellect** | 10 (+0) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 8 (-1) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Agility +4 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | Stealth +4 |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Jagged Shank:** +1 to hit, range 1, 1d4-1 piercing. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser darts in and jabs (insert target) with a jagged shank!* **Poison Gas Vial:** +4 to hit, range 3, 1d4 piercing + 1d4 poison; target must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or become **Poisoned** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser hurls a glass vial of poisonous gas at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Stoppered Gas Vial** (10%) |
| **Comments** | “Breathe deep, pretty thing!”, “Cough for me!”, “Rot in your chest, slow and wet!”, “Glass for the lungs, gas for the eyes!”, “Heh—listen to ’em choke!”, “Boss says don’t waste the good fumes!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Gasser has a chance to drop:
- **Stoppered Gas Vial** — 10%

### Stoppered Gas Vial Summary
A cloudy glass vial stoppered with black wax, still swirling with a foul green vapor that stings the eyes.
- non-magical
- worth 30 gp

### Tactics Note
Boggart Gassers prefer to fight from the rear ranks, hurling poison into weakened or tightly packed enemies while larger boggarts keep victims pinned in place.
